articles.title: Boston City Council approves $1.7 billion schools budget that cuts hundreds of jobs
articles.description:
The council chose to delay a vote on Mayor Wu's contentious operating budget proposal, saying they need more time to work through amendments.

The post Boston City Council approves $1.7 billion schools budget that cuts hundreds of jobs appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston City Council approved a $1.7 billion budget for schools.
The budget cuts hundreds of jobs affecting local education staff.
Council delayed voting on Mayor Wu's operating budget proposal for further amendments.

articles.title: Boozy ice cream is coming to Boston
articles.description:
New York-based Tipsy Scoop plans to open a South End location next year and will begin catering events and offering delivery in the meantime.

The post Boozy ice cream is coming to Boston appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Tipsy Scoop will open a new location in Boston's South End in 2024.
The New York-based company will start catering events and offering delivery services soon.
Boston residents can soon enjoy boozy ice cream, enhancing local dessert options.

articles.title: Kevin McHale says Larry Bird would light up the modern NBA like a ‘five-alarm fire’
articles.description:
“I just laugh at these people today,” McHale said of modern players who doubt Bird's greatness.

The post Kevin McHale says Larry Bird would light up the modern NBA like a ‘five-alarm fire’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kevin McHale praises Larry Bird's potential impact in today's NBA during a recent interview.
McHale criticizes modern players doubting Bird's greatness, emphasizing his unmatched skills.
Boston fans celebrate Bird's legacy as McHale reinforces his significance in Celtics history.

articles.title: Boston Police Officer Nicholas O’Malley pleads not guilty to manslaughter in deadly Roxbury shooting
articles.description:
“There is no police officer in this country that would have acted differently,” O’Malley’s defense attorney alleged.

The post Boston Police Officer Nicholas O’Malley pleads not guilty to manslaughter in deadly Roxbury shooting appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Officer Nicholas O’Malley pleads not guilty to manslaughter in Roxbury shooting case.
O'Malley's defense claims no officer would have acted differently during the incident.
The case raises concerns about police conduct and community safety in Boston.

articles.title: Embrace Boston to open a new civic space in Downtown Crossing
articles.description:
The nonprofit has acquired a 35,000-square-foot property just minutes from the Embrace monument on Boston Common.

The post Embrace Boston to open a new civic space in Downtown Crossing appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Embrace Boston acquired a 35,000-square-foot property in Downtown Crossing.
The new civic space is located minutes from the Embrace monument on Boston Common.
This initiative aims to enhance community engagement and public gatherings in Boston.

articles.title: Portland Hearts of Pine to add a women’s soccer team
articles.description:
The team will play at Fitzpatrick Stadium, where the Hearts of Pine play their home matches.

The post Portland Hearts of Pine to add a women’s soccer team appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Portland Hearts of Pine will introduce a women's soccer team in 2024.
The team will play at Fitzpatrick Stadium, enhancing local sports offerings.
This addition aims to promote women's sports and community engagement in Portland.
